comment_text.sn42.1:1.2: “Fury” (caṇḍa) is a descriptive epithet used as a nickname. Such nicknames were a feature of the culture. Sometimes, texts invent a character as a vehicle for the teaching; such artifice may well have been intentional and received as such. By translating such names where appropriate, I aim to call attention to the way names are used to add a dimension of meaning. | Gāmaṇi is literally a “village headman”. Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa 5.4.4.18 establishes that the village headman ranks beneath the sūta (“equerry”, see SN 19.8:1.4 and note). However, it is used for a chief in general: in the next Sutta it is a “dancing master”, and in the one after that, a military rank.

root_text.sn42.1:1.2: Atha kho caṇḍo gāmaṇi yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho caṇḍo gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.1:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo yena midhekacco caṇḍo caṇḍotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn42.1:1.4: Ko pana, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo yena midhekacco sorato soratotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchatī”ti?
root_text.sn42.1:1.6: Rāgassa appahīnattā pare kopenti, parehi kopiyamāno kopaṁ pātukaroti.
root_text.sn42.1:1.9: Dosassa appahīnattā pare kopenti, parehi kopiyamāno kopaṁ pātukaroti.
root_text.sn42.1:1.12: Mohassa appahīnattā pare kopenti, parehi kopiyamāno kopaṁ pātukaroti.
root_text.sn42.1:1.14: Ayaṁ kho, gāmaṇi, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo yena midhekacco caṇḍo caṇḍotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn42.1:2.1: Idha pana, gāmaṇi, ekaccassa rāgo pahīno hoti.
root_text.sn42.1:2.2: Rāgassa pahīnattā pare na kopenti, parehi kopiyamāno kopaṁ na pātukaroti.
root_text.sn42.1:2.5: Dosassa pahīnattā pare na kopenti, parehi kopiyamāno kopaṁ na pātukaroti.
root_text.sn42.1:2.8: Mohassa pahīnattā pare na kopenti, parehi kopiyamāno kopaṁ na pātukaroti.
root_text.sn42.1:2.10: Ayaṁ kho, gāmaṇi, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena midhekacco sorato soratotveva saṅkhaṁ gacchatī”ti.
root_text.sn42.1:3.1: Evaṁ vutte, caṇḍo gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.1:3.3: Seyyathāpi, bhante, nikkujjitaṁ vā ukkujjeyya, paṭicchannaṁ vā vivareyya, mūḷhassa vā maggaṁ ācikkheyya, andhakāre vā telapajjotaṁ dhāreyya: ‘cakkhumanto rūpāni dakkhantī’ti; evamevaṁ bhagavatā anekapariyāyena dhammo pakāsito.
root_text.sn42.1:3.4: Esāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavantaṁ saraṇaṁ gacchāmi dhammañca bhikkhusaṅghañca.
root_text.sn42.1:3.5: Upāsakaṁ maṁ bhagavā dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn42.1:1.2: Then Fury the chief went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn42.1:1.3: “What is the cause, sir, what is the reason why some people are regarded as furious,
translation_text.sn42.1:1.4: while others are regarded as gentle?”
translation_text.sn42.1:1.5: “Take someone who hasn’t given up greed.
translation_text.sn42.1:1.6: So they get annoyed by others, and they show it.
translation_text.sn42.1:1.9: So they get annoyed by others, and they show it.
translation_text.sn42.1:1.12: So they get annoyed by others, and they show it.
translation_text.sn42.1:1.14: This is the cause, this is the reason why some people are regarded as furious.
translation_text.sn42.1:2.1: But take someone who has given up greed.
translation_text.sn42.1:2.2: So they don’t get annoyed by others, and don’t show it.
translation_text.sn42.1:2.5: So they don’t get annoyed by others, and don’t show it.
translation_text.sn42.1:2.8: So they don’t get annoyed by others, and don’t show it.
translation_text.sn42.1:2.10: This is the cause, this is the reason why some people are regarded as gentle.”
translation_text.sn42.1:3.1: When he said this, Fury the chief said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn42.1:3.3: As if he were righting the overturned, or revealing the hidden, or pointing out the path to the lost, or lighting a lamp in the dark so people with clear eyes can see what’s there, the Buddha has made the teaching clear in many ways.
translation_text.sn42.1:3.4: I go for refuge to the Buddha, to the teaching, and to the mendicant Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn42.1:3.5: From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.sn42.1:1.3: yena midhekacco caṇḍo caṇḍotveva → yena midhekacco caṇḍoteva (bj, pts1ed)
variant_text.sn42.1:1.4: yena midhekacco sorato soratotveva → yena midhekacco suratoteva (bj); yena midhekacco sūratoteva (pts1ed)
